TRANSCRIPT
Now we're going to look at the prelaunch video #2, PLC #2, of Will Hamilton. This is his
Tennis Ninja launch and I went through these details in the first video when I broke down his
first PLC, but he had a basically 17,000-person list, did $168,000 in sales, which is wonderful
and Will has quickly gotten better with these launches. When he did this launch he'd only
owned Product Launch Formula for less than a year, so a highly successful launch.
A few things before we dig into this and go through the video. First of all, one of the things
Will does in his videos really, really well, is he builds authority and part of his positioning is
his access to the pros and his access as the credentialed media at the top level of the game,
but the thing to remember is that he created that authority through his launches. He wasn't a
member of the media. He wasn't a recognized person until he started doing these launches
and started having real success, starting to build up a lot of views on YouTube. So he basically
created this authority through his launches, so it's a big thing to remember.
Now you should definitely watch his PLC #1, the video I shot about that, I broke that down. I
thought his PLC #1 was brilliant. He just did a fantastic job of building authority and
describing what this entire video course was going to be about and why you need it, as a
tennis player you would need to watch this entire video series. So he did an incredibly
wonderful job.
This second video I don't think is quite as powerful and it's not unusual that you're not always
going to hit a home run, you're not always going to do your absolute best work or have your
absolute best video and I think in every launch I've had, I've had one video that stood head
and shoulders above the others and one that might not be as good. So I don't think this one is
quite up to the standards of the first video but it's still very strong.
Where this one is really, really strong is in the teaching portion. I think there is excellent
teaching in here. There's teaching as I was going through this video, as a tennis player myself,
as I was going through this video preparing to share with you what's going on I found myself
watching the content and not wanting to fast forward through the content because it's really,
really solid.
So I think the teaching portion, the content portion in the middle is particularly strong. I
think the open is not quite as strong and the close probably wasn't quite as strong.
There isn't as much foreshadowing of the next video throughout here, but still it's a very good

offense and we're going to specifically define how you know what state
